plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth about Jon Arryn's death and protect the realm
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister conspiracy, court politics, his own moral inflexibility
plotlines.stakes: his life, his family's safety, the stability of the realm

plotlines.name: Cersei: Power
plotlines.goal: protect her children's claim to the throne and maintain Lannister control
plotlines.obstacle: Ned's investigation, Robert's potential discovery of the truth
plotlines.stakes: her children's lives, her own execution for treason

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Dragons
plotlines.goal: transform from powerless exile into a true queen
plotlines.obstacle: Dothraki culture, her brother's control, Drogo's death
plotlines.stakes: death, permanent exile, loss of identity

plotlines.name: Jon: Belonging
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose at the Night's Watch
plotlines.obstacle: his privileged background, the reality of the Watch, divided loyalties
plotlines.stakes: dishonor, abandonment of duty, loss of identity

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive the accusations and navigate family conflicts
plotlines.obstacle: false accusations, hostile captors, family expectations

plotlines.name: Catelyn: Vengeance
plotlines.goal: protect her family and seek justice for the attack on Bran
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister power, political complexity, distance from family

plotlines.name: Robb: War
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and defend the North
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister military strength, political alliances, inexperience
plotlines.stakes: his father's life, northern independence, family honor

plotlines.name: Sansa: Innocence
plotlines.goal: navigate court life and maintain her dreams of queenhood
plotlines.obstacle: brutal political reality, Joffrey's cruelty, family conflict
plotlines.stakes: her life, her family's safety, loss of innocence

plotlines.name: Arya: Identity
plotlines.goal: maintain her true self while learning to survive
plotlines.obstacle: gender expectations, court restrictions, family separation
plotlines.stakes: loss of identity, death, permanent separation from family

plotlines.name: Night's Watch: Supernatural Threat
plotlines.goal: investigate and defend against the supernatural threat beyond the Wall
plotlines.obstacle: disbelief, limited resources, unknown enemy capabilities
plotlines.stakes: the realm's survival, the Watch's purpose

events.event: Three Night's Watch rangers discover wildlings slaughtered in ritual pattern by White Walkers
White Walker kills two rangers, Will flees south
Ned executes deserter Will for abandoning his post, bringing his sons to witness justice
Stark children find dead direwolf with pups, each child claims one including Jon's albino Ghost
Raven brings news that Jon Arryn has died and King Robert rides north
King Robert arrives at Winterfell with the Lannisters and his court
Robert visits Lyanna's crypt and asks Ned to serve as Hand of the King
Robert proposes marriage between Joffrey and Sansa Stark
Catelyn urges Ned to be cautious about Robert's offer
Viserys arranges Daenerys's marriage to Khal Drogo in exchange for his army
Daenerys submits to the marriage arrangement despite her terror
Jorah Mormont presents himself to Daenerys and offers her Westerosi history books
Catelyn receives secret letter from Lysa claiming Lannisters murdered Jon Arryn
Ned decides he must go south to investigate Jon Arryn's death
Bran discovers Cersei and Jaime in sexual embrace while climbing
Jaime pushes Bran from the window to protect their secret
Maester Luwin tells Catelyn and Robb that Bran will never walk again if he lives
Catelyn refuses to leave Bran's bedside, neglecting Winterfell's affairs
Jaime expresses unease to Cersei about what Bran might remember
Cersei dismisses the risk, confident a child's word means nothing against theirs
Jon decides to join the Night's Watch and travels north with Tyrion
Jon gives Arya a small sword she names Needle
Jon says farewell to the unconscious Bran
Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya
Sansa and Joffrey walk together along the Trident river
They encounter Arya sparring with the butcher's boy Mycah
Joffrey threatens Mycah with his sword
Arya's direwolf Nymeria bites Joffrey's arm in defense
Arya drives Nymeria away to save the wolf from punishment
Robert holds court on the matter and Cersei demands a direwolf be killed
Robert orders Sansa's innocent direwolf Lady executed instead
Ned carries out the execution sentence himself
The Hound hunts down and kills the butcher's boy
Daenerys struggles with painful nights with Drogo and grueling days of riding
Jorah rides alongside Daenerys and explains Dothraki customs
Daenerys learns from her handmaiden how to assert herself with Drogo
Daenerys faces Drogo as an equal rather than a victim, shifting their marriage dynamic
An assassin enters Bran's room with a Valyrian steel dagger to kill him
Catelyn fights the assassin bare-handed until Bran's direwolf kills him
Catelyn resolves to ride south secretly to warn Ned about the Lannister threat
Jon Snow arrives at Castle Black and discovers most recruits are criminals and outcasts
Jon's superior combat training makes him dominant in practice, earning resentment from other recruits
Tyrion advises Jon to stop acting like a privileged lord's son and help the weaker boys
Jon takes Tyrion's advice and begins helping the weaker recruits train
Ned arrives in King's Landing and takes his seat on the Small Council
Ned meets Littlefinger, Varys, and Grand Maester Pycelle on the Small Council
Ned learns the Crown owes millions to the Lannisters and Iron Bank due to Robert's reckless spending
Robert demands a lavish tournament in Ned's honor despite Ned's objections about expense
Catelyn arrives in King's Landing in secret and Littlefinger arranges a private meeting with Ned
Catelyn shows Ned the Valyrian steel dagger used in the attempt on Bran's life
Littlefinger identifies the dagger as one he lost to Tyrion Lannister in a bet
Ned and Catelyn agree she must return north while he investigates from within the court
Arya chases cats through the Red Keep and stumbles into the cellar
Arya overhears two shadowy men discussing Stark-Lannister tension and the need to delay war
Arya fails to convince her father of the significance of what she overheard
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt so she can explore on foot, defying Viserys
Viserys strikes Daenerys for her defiance
Daenerys's Dothraki guards intervene and she allows them to humiliate Viserys
Viserys realizes his sister is no longer under his control and the Dothraki respect her
Ned visits the blacksmith's apprentice Gendry, who bears a striking resemblance to young Robert Baratheon
Ned realizes Robert has bastard children throughout King's Landing, all with black hair
Ned obtains the genealogical history book that Jon Arryn was studying before his death
Tyrion arrives at Winterfell and gives Bran designs for a special saddle to help him ride despite his paralysis
Robb receives Tyrion coldly, suspicious of all Lannisters
Daenerys discovers she is pregnant with Drogo's child
Jorah tells Daenerys privately that a Targaryen heir changes the political calculation in Westeros
Daenerys announces her pregnancy to Drogo, who declares the child will be the Stallion Who Mounts the World
Viserys grows increasingly bitter that the Dothraki show him no deference
Ser Gregor Clegane kills Ser Hugh of the Vale during the tournament joust
Littlefinger tells Sansa the story of how Gregor burned his brother the Hound's face as a child
Ned suspects Ser Hugh's death was not accidental and that someone is eliminating Jon Arryn's witnesses
Catelyn recognizes Tyrion among travelers at the Crossroads Inn
Catelyn calls on her father's bannermen and arrests Tyrion for the attempted murder of Bran
Catelyn takes Tyrion east toward the Eyrie instead of south to King's Landing to avoid Lannister pursuit
Robert receives word that Daenerys is pregnant and orders her assassination
Ned argues fiercely against murdering Daenerys and her unborn child
Robert refuses to relent on the assassination order
Ned resigns as Hand of the King in protest
Ned announces he will return to Winterfell
Varys warns Ned that his investigation has made him a target
Varys reveals that Jon Arryn was poisoned
Catelyn's party is ambushed by hill tribes on the road to the Eyrie
Tyrion fights alongside his captors to survive the ambush
The party reaches the Eyrie safely
Lysa Arryn imprisons Tyrion in the sky cells
Tyrion discovers Lysa has already convicted him in her mind
Jon and his fellow recruits prepare to take their vows at Castle Black
Jon is assigned to the stewards rather than the rangers
Sam explains that steward assignment means grooming for command
Arya practices sword training with Syrio Forel
Ned watches Arya's lesson and his expression darkens at the sound of clashing swords
Littlefinger takes Ned to a brothel to see another of Robert's bastards
Ned realizes all Baratheon children have black hair while Cersei's are golden-haired
Ned discovers Joffrey, Myrcella, and Tommen are products of Cersei and Jaime's incest
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets with armed men after learning of Tyrion's arrest
Jaime's soldiers kill Ned's guards in the street fight
Jaime personally wounds Ned before riding away demanding Tyrion's release
Ned wakes from his injuries to find Robert has returned from hunting and demands he resume duties as Hand
Robert threatens to give the Hand position to Jaime if Ned refuses
Ned reluctantly agrees to resume his duties as Hand
Robert rides out hunting again, leaving Ned to sit the Iron Throne
Villagers petition the court reporting Ser Gregor Clegane's raids and massacres in the Riverlands
Ned recognizes this as Lannister provocation to draw the Tullys into conflict
Ned sends Lord Beric Dondarrion to bring Gregor to justice and summons Tywin Lannister to court
Tyrion demands a trial by combat rather than face Lysa's biased judgment
Tyrion recruits the sellsword Bronn to fight as his champion
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen using speed and cunning against armored strength
Lysa is forced to release Tyrion, who walks out free with Bronn at his side
Viserys arrives drunk at a Dothraki feast and draws his sword in Vaes Dothrak where blades are forbidden
Viserys threatens Daenerys and demands the crown Drogo promised him
Drogo melts gold and pours it over Viserys's head, killing him with a golden crown
Daenerys watches her brother die without flinching and declares he was no dragon
Jorah observes Daenerys's transformation with admiration and concern
Jon Snow and Sam take their vows before a heart tree beyond the Wall
Jon's direwolf Ghost emerges carrying a severed human hand, alerting the Watch that the dead are stirring
Ned studies the genealogy book and confirms every Baratheon lord has produced black-haired children
Ned holds the secret that could destroy the Lannisters but wrestles with how to use it without starting a war
Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children are Jaime's and offering her a chance to flee
Cersei coldly responds that in the game of thrones you win or you die, refusing to flee
Robert is brought back mortally wounded by a boar during the hunt
On his deathbed, Robert dictates his will naming Ned Protector of the Realm
Ned writes 'rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in Robert's will
Robert dies
Ned sends a letter to Stannis at Dragonstone informing him of the truth about Cersei's children
Ned asks Littlefinger to secure the City Watch's loyalty
Littlefinger advises Ned to make peace with the Lannisters and rule through Joffrey, but Ned refuses
Renly offers Ned backing of his household guard to seize Joffrey before the Lannisters can act
Ned refuses Renly's offer, unwilling to frighten a child with soldiers
Renly flees King's Landing
A wine merchant tries to poison Daenerys on Robert's orders
Jorah Mormont intervenes and exposes the assassination plot
The assassination attempt enrages Drogo, who vows to cross the Narrow Sea and conquer Westeros
Two dead rangers' bodies are brought inside Castle Black
The corpses reanimate as wights in the night
Jon fights a wight attacking the Lord Commander and saves his life by setting it on fire
Ned enters the throne room to present Robert's will naming him Protector of the Realm
Cersei tears up the will and declares Joffrey king
On Littlefinger's signal, the gold cloaks turn on Ned's men, killing his household guard
Littlefinger puts a dagger to Ned's throat, telling him he warned not to trust him
Ned is arrested and imprisoned in the dungeons beneath the Red Keep
Sansa is kept in her chambers under Lannister guard
Syrio Forel fights the Lannister soldiers with a wooden practice sword, buying Arya time to flee
Syrio is overwhelmed and presumably killed
Arya recovers Needle and hides in the streets of King's Landing
Drogo's khalasar raids a village to fund the invasion of Westeros
Daenerys orders the Dothraki to stop raping the captive women, taking them under her protection
Mago challenges Daenerys's order, angering Drogo's warriors
Drogo fights Mago in single combat, killing him bare-handed despite taking a chest wound
Mirri Maz Duur offers to treat Drogo's wound, and Daenerys accepts her help
Lord Commander Mormont rewards Jon with Longclaw for saving his life from the wight
Jon receives word that his father has been arrested for treason and attempts to desert
Sam, Grenn, and Pyp convince Jon to honor his vow and return
Tyrion recruits the hill tribes by promising Tywin's gold and weapons
Tyrion arrives at Tywin's war camp where Tywin is assembling an army
Robb Stark calls the northern bannermen to war and marches south with twenty thousand men to rescue his father
Catelyn negotiates passage at the Twins by promising Robb will marry a Frey daughter and Arya will marry a Frey son
Robb splits his forces, sending a feint toward Tywin's army while leading the main force to Riverrun
The feint engages Tywin's forces in the Battle of the Green Fork
Tyrion fights alongside the hill tribes and is knocked unconscious early in the fighting
Tywin wins the battle but realizes too late that the main Stark army was not there
Robb's force ambushes Jaime's army at the Battle of the Whispering Wood and wins decisively
Robb personally captures Jaime Lannister
Catelyn confronts the captive Jaime, who taunts her but cannot deny his vulnerability
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon and urges him to confess to treason for Sansa's sake
Ned agrees to lie and confess for his daughter's sake
Ned is brought before the crowd at the Great Sept and publicly confesses to treason
Joffrey orders Ned's execution despite Cersei and the Small Council expecting mercy
Ser Ilyn Payne beheads Ned Stark with his own sword Ice
Sansa screams as her father is executed
Yoren grabs Arya from the crowd, shields her eyes and pulls her away
Drogo's wound festers into a deadly infection
Daenerys desperately asks Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo
Mirri agrees to the blood magic but warns that only death pays for life
Daenerys goes into premature labor and is carried into the tent during the dark magic ritual
Daenerys wakes to find Drogo alive but in a vegetative state, his mind destroyed by Mirri's blood magic
Daenerys discovers her son was stillborn and monstrous, the death that paid for Drogo's life
Mirri reveals she acted deliberately as revenge, telling Daenerys she saved no one
Most of the khalasar abandons Daenerys for a new khal, leaving her with only loyal followers
Jorah urges Daenerys to flee east, but she chooses a different path
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at Ned's head mounted on a spike
Sansa nearly pushes Joffrey from the rampart but the Hound stops her
The Hound quietly wipes blood from Sansa's lip after Joffrey has a guard strike her
Arya travels north disguised as a boy named Arry with Yoren and Night's Watch recruits
Gendry, Robert's unrecognized bastard, unknowingly flees the Lannisters among the recruits
Robb's bannermen debate their next move at the Twins
Robb proposes marching south to avenge Ned but cannot serve a Baratheon king
Greatjon Umber draws his sword and declares Robb the King in the North
The northern lords kneel and take up the chant, crowning Robb king
Catelyn visits captive Jaime and strikes him for what the Lannisters have done
Lord Commander Mormont tells Jon his duty lies with the Watch, not the war of kings
Mormont announces a great ranging to find Benjen and investigate the White Walkers
Jon rides out with the Night's Watch into the haunted forest
Daenerys builds a funeral pyre for Drogo and places her dragon eggs on it
Daenerys ties Mirri Maz Duur to the flames as a final sacrifice
Daenerys walks into the fire herself over Jorah's protests
Daenerys emerges unburned with three living dragons, the first in over a century
The remaining Dothraki kneel before Daenerys as she holds the dragons
